name: Tamale Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 can black beans, rinsed and drained (or about 1 1/2 cups cooked dried black beans)
1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds, toasted and cracked (or just under 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin)
1/2 teaspoon coriander seeds, toasted and cracked (or under 1/2 teaspoon ground coriander)
2 small carrots, chopped (or 1 large carrot, chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the spices and sauté the carrots in vinegar or your preferred cooking liquid until tender.
2. Add the jalapeno, black beans, and remaining spices to the sautéed carrots. Cook for about 3 minutes, then stir in the salsa.
3. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook the bean mixture for another minute.
4. In a separate pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil with a dash of salt.
5. Gradually sprinkle the corn meal into the boiling water while stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
6. Continue adding corn meal until you have a thick porridge resembling grits or cream of wheat.
7. Stir in the chili powder and remove the bean mixture from heat if the carrots are tender.
8. Spread half of the corn meal mixture into the bottom of a large loaf pan or small baking dish.
9. Layer the bean mixture on top of the corn batter.
10. Cover with the remaining corn meal mixture, forming a top crust.
11. Bake at 400°F for 30 minutes or until the top corn meal batter is firmly set.
12. Garnish with fresh tomatoes, cilantro, scallions, hot sauce, etc., if desired.
